title: Agent Control Contract — Run-Outcome Report
description: The machine-readable terminal-outcome object a once/loop/schedule-bounded agent run writes at the terminal transition. It is the durable, structured backend for `kubectl agents results`: a Job's pod is gone seconds after exit, so the full outcome (terminal status, exit code, usage, timings, a pointer to the distilled result, the trace id) is captured here, not inferred from a vanished pod. Built ONCE, at the terminal transition, from what the supervisor knows; serialized before the proc.exit log line. Two delivery surfaces, both optional and off for a bare CLI run: a report file written atomically (temp + rename) at the terminal transition (env AGENT_REPORT_FILE; flag --report-file PATH), and the served `agent://run/{run_id}` resource frozen to this schema (which embeds this object under the `report` key). Reactive daemons emit NO report — they have no single terminal outcome; their per-reaction outcomes live in metrics and the event stream. Telemetry never crashes the run: a failed report-file write logs report.write.fail (warn) and the run STILL exits with the correct exit code — the exit code is the floor contract and never depends on the report landing. This object is OPEN/additive-tolerant: a consumer MUST ignore unknown fields (a new field is a minor bump of report_schema) and refuse only an unknown report_schema MAJOR. SIBLING NOTE — events + correlation + trace: this report is the post-mortem of a run whose live narrative is the JSON-lines event stream (agent://events, schema events.schema.json). The two share the SAME run_id / trace_id correlation. For self-containment of codegen consumers, the event-line shape, the correlation tuple (run_id + agent_id + agent_path + W3C trace_id/span_id/parent_span_id), and the closed v1 event vocabulary are carried here as reference-only $defs ($defs.correlation, $defs.event_line, $defs.event_name_v1); they are NOT part of the report object and mirror the event stream defined in events.schema.json.

x-contract.versioning: report_schema major.minor; new field => minor (consumer tolerates via additionalProperties); removed/renamed/narrowed field => major (consumer branches or refuses). Surface absent in the manifest => the binary did not build the report path; the consumer degrades to exit-code + logs.

sibling_artifacts.events: contract/v1/events.schema.json (agent://events live window; same run_id/trace_id correlation)
sibling_artifacts.common_lift: terminal_status + exit_code SHOULD lift to a shared common.schema.json (co-used by the metrics status label and the exit-code-contract surface); inlined here to avoid colliding with the sibling extractions.

report_schema.description: Frozen schema version major.minor. Current 1.0. A breaking field change bumps the major; the consumer refuses an unknown major and tolerates an unknown minor.

run_id.description: The run_id — the unit of work, a ULID, stable across the whole process tree. Stable across a retried Job when the operator sets AGENT_RUN_ID, so a retried unit's reports collapse to one. This is the primary correlation key joining the report to the event stream (see $defs.correlation).

instance.description: Downward-API instance identity (e.g. pod name, from metadata.name via AGENT_POD_NAME) when present. OMITTED ENTIRELY when absent — never null. A consumer MUST model this as optional and NOT expect a null value.

mode.description: Execution shape that produced this report. NEVER `reactive` — reactive daemons emit no report. Closed set: the report-emitting modes only.

has_usable_partial.description: Whether the run produced usable partial output. Drives the exit-code 3-vs-7 split (EXIT_PARTIAL vs EXIT_BUDGET) the supervisor decides from the result body, not from a distinct status; "partial" is NOT a terminal status — it is a result-body property.

usage.description: Per-run usage roll-up. Token honesty: absence is 0, NEVER an estimate — a gateway that omits usage leaves these at 0 so a cost reader stays trustworthy. The contract emits TOKENS, never currency; cost = tokens × a price table the consumer owns (no pricing in the data plane).

tokens_in.description: Cumulative input tokens from the intelligence usage.

steps.description: Loop steps across the whole tree.

subagents.description: Total subagents spawned in the run.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock run duration in milliseconds (ended_at - started_at), clamped to 0 on a non-monotonic clock — never negative.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC start timestamp (millisecond precision; always UTC, never local time).

distillate_ref.description: Handle where the distilled result BODY lives; `agent://subagent/0/result`. It POINTS, it does not embed — the report stays small and bounded; the body is read on demand (and for a CLI run is also on stdout). There is no contract guarantee that this handle is re-readable AFTER the process exits: a once-mode result can be lost if the relay/collector blinks. Until a re-read-after-exit primitive exists, the durable copy is the report file itself, and the body is durable only via stdout capture.

refusals.description: Per-run roll-up of refusal/guard-trip counts over the closed reason set. The metric counters are fleet-cumulative; this is THIS run, so a reader shows "this run hit 1 depth refusal" with no metrics query. Reasons not hit this run stay 0. additionalProperties:true so a new bounded reason (additive, minor bump) is tolerated.

terminal_status.description: The terminal-status string — the precise reason the run stopped, drawn from a single authoritative closed vocabulary; the same closed vocabulary is the `status` label domain of the run-lifecycle counter (agent_runs_total{status}) in the metrics registry. CLOSED set: adding a status is additive (minor bump), removing/renaming one is breaking (major bump). This $def SHOULD be lifted to a shared common.schema.json so the report, the metrics schema, and the A2A TerminalStatus->TaskState mapping reference one definition. Distinct from exit_code, which is the coarse, lossy projection: completed->0; refused->5; cancelled (clean drain)->0; crashed->1; deadline->124; an exhausted_*/stalled/loop_detected run projects to 3 when has_usable_partial else 7.

exit_code.description: The process exit code — the coarse projection of `status`, frozen as the `exit_codes` contract surfaced at surfaces.exit_codes. Both `status` and `exit_code` are present so a reader sees the precise reason AND can author exit-code policy. Reachable by a once/Job run: 0/1/3/4/5/6/7/124 (+ kernel 137). 2 (EXIT_USAGE) is not reachable from a report — config validation fails before any run. 137 (OOM/kubelet SIGKILL) and 143 (ungraceful SIGTERM) are kernel-set and never returned by the binary — a clean drain returns 0, not 143 — kept in the enum for completeness.

x-codes.2: EXIT_USAGE (config error; not reachable from a report) — intent terminal

trace_id.description: W3C trace-context trace id, 16 bytes as 32 lowercase hex chars. Stitches a multi-pod flow into one trace; the report's tie into the correlation tuple ($defs.correlation). OMITTED when no trace exists — never null. Ingested-or-minted on the run; a consumer sets it at the flow root via AGENT_TRACEPARENT or an inbound `_meta.traceparent`, and every downstream pod stitches in.

correlation.description: SIBLING/REFERENCE-ONLY (not part of the report object). The correlation tuple that joins this report to the live event stream (events.schema.json) and to cross-pod traces. run_id is the unit of work (matches report.run_id); agent_id + agent_path locate the emitting node within the tree; the W3C trace_id/span_id/parent_span_id stitch across pods. A consumer correlates `kubectl agents results <run_id>` (this report) with `agent://events?...` lines sharing the same run_id, and with OTel spans sharing the same trace_id.

run_id.description: ULID for the whole invocation; matches report.run_id.

agent_id.description: Emitting process id; the supervisor uses the reserved `sup`/`root`.

agent_path.description: Dotted tree path (`0`, `0.2`, `0.2.1`); prefix-scopes a subtree with no backend join.

span_id.description: W3C span id, 8 bytes as 16 lowercase hex; present in-span.

parent_span_id.description: Parent W3C span id, 8 bytes as 16 lowercase hex; present in-span.

event_line.description: SIBLING/REFERENCE-ONLY (not part of the report object; duplicated from events.schema.json for codegen self-containment). One JSON-lines telemetry line. Event-specific fields (tool, server, tokens_in/tokens_out, resource_uri, route, limit, value, cap, child_agent_id, child_path, depth, signal, finish_reason, result_bytes, ...) ride additionalProperties keyed by the event name — a consumer ignores fields it does not recognise. The live agent://events window adds a monotonic ring `seq` on top of this line shape (see events.schema.json).

ts.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp, millisecond precision. Always UTC.

event.description: Dotted event type — the primary index/filter key. A small CLOSED v1 vocabulary (see $defs.event_name_v1) BUT additive-tolerant: a new event name is a minor change, so this is typed `string` and a consumer MUST tolerate an unrecognised name (enforcing the enum would break additive tolerance).

run_id.description: ULID for the whole invocation, stable across the tree.

agent_path.description: Dotted tree path; prefix scopes a subtree.

pid.description: OS process id — joins the log tree to the OS pstree.

span_id.description: W3C span id, 8 bytes as 16 lowercase hex. Present in-span.

parent_span_id.description: Parent span id, 8 bytes as 16 lowercase hex. Present in-span.

dur_ms.description: Duration in milliseconds, present on `*.end`/`*.result` events.

err.description: Structured error, present on error events — NEVER a stringified stack.

msg.description: Optional short human string — never the structured payload.

event_name_v1.description: SIBLING/REFERENCE-ONLY. The frozen v1 closed event vocabulary: 19 supervisor/lifecycle + 8 agentic-loop = 27 names. Provided for codegen/reference; NOT enforced on the event_line.event field, because a new name is additive and consumers must tolerate it. `comp:"mcp"` reuses these names; it introduces none. The report-write events (`report.written`/`report.write.fail`) SHOULD be treated as additive members beyond the 27.
